summary:
Fluorsopy is essential for accurately navigating and positioning during endoscopic ureteric stone retrieval because it provides real-time visualization critical for guiding catheters through the complex anatomy of the upper urinary tract. This role aligns directly with established standards where fluoroscopy is classified under diagnostic procedures related to the urinary system.  

Final Answer: Yes

The summary must be strictly limited to two sentences only; no additional content or explanation should appear beyond these constraints.

Fluoroscopy is essential for precise guidance and navigation during endoscopic retrieval of lower ureteric stones by providing real-time visualization of the urinary tract. It is considered an indispensable component due to its integration into standardized procedural protocols involving urological interventions.
